page: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive, GPU-accelerated server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.4 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance bundles four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs, delivering a total of 179 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the g6e.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model inference. Prompt processing and text generation speeds across small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top 10 percent of tested systems. CPU performance is strong, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics, as well as Passmark CPU and encryption tests, placing in the top 25 percent. Graphics-related tasks also show strong capabilities, with Geekbench ray tracing and image processing benchmarks scoring in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth for cached read-only small blocks is strong, while uncached large block read and write operations perform in the average tier.

Given its dense hardware profile, the instance offers high resource density by combining substantial GPU acceleration, high-speed local NVMe storage, and a robust memory footprint. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring simultaneous high-throughput storage access and parallel GPU computation, avoiding the need for separate storage attachments. The instance is optimized for demanding workloads such as large language model inference, real-time ray tracing, image processing, and high-performance database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated, graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs from dedicated 3.4 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processors, 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD. For parallel processing, it bundles four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM, supported by a 100 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in LLM inference for prompt processing and text generation across 135M, 7B, and 70B models. CPU and memory benchmarks, including stress-ng and Passmark, rank in the top 25 percent, alongside strong ray tracing and image processing capabilities. This high resource density makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale AI inference, graphics rendering, and data-intensive database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, and four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB VRAM for high-performance AI inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and four NVIDIA L40S GPUs for top-tier LLM inference workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance combining four NVIDIA L40S accelerators, local NVMe storage, and top-tier large language model inference performance.
bullet_points: Equipped with four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPUs providing 179 GB of total VRAM
Features 48 vCPUs via dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processors and 384 GB of DDR4 memory
Includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
Optimized for AI model serving, real-time ray tracing, and data-intensive workloads
